title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They will announce the winner of the competition tomorrow.
‘I didn't break the vase,’ said the little boy.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs (deny) followed by the gerund.

questions.firstSentence: I was only able to solve the puzzle because you gave me a hint.
questions.beforeGap: I wouldn't have been able to solve the puzzle
questions.explanation: Tests conditional meaning using 'without' as a replacement for 'if it hadn't been for'.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure that Mark was at the party last night.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for certain past deduction.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't realize who she was until she started speaking.
questions.afterGap: she started speaking that I realized who she was.
questions.explanation: Tests 'It was only when...' cleft sentences.

questions.firstSentence: I find it difficult to get up early in the morning.
questions.explanation: Tests 'be used to' + gerund for familiarity.
